text:
There once was an owl who liked to count things. She liked counting bugs. She liked counting owls. But most of all, she loved counting apples.

One morning she swooped down from her lofty branch. There were dozens! She flew down. Whooo! They were falling fast.

Now, owls aren't usually good at falling things.

They crashed into the swamp pond.


That night an apple hawk swooped down.

That night!

Just a single red apple.

That's all it took. The hawk had a laugh.

Just one.

The owl looked at her own scale.

Maybe seven.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
